compliantItems.element: Element b - Sliding Fee Discount Program Policies
compliantItems.requirement: The health center has board-approved policy(ies) for its sliding fee discount program that apply uniformly to all patients and address the following areas:
compliantItems.evidence: "Based on Governing Board-approved policies, the sliding fee discount program applies uniformly to all patients... The Board-approved sliding fee discount program policy defines income as modified adjusted gross income (MAGI)... The definition of family size is based on Federal Poverty Guidelines... Patient registration procedures include assessing/reassessing all patients for discount eligibility based only on income and family size... The sliding fee discount schedule scale in Attachment 10 demonstrates that VHHP provides discounts and adjusts charges for patients in four pay classes... VHHP does not have nominal fees for patients with incomes at or below 100% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines."
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Project Narrative - Response Section; Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance
compliantItems.reasoning: The application provides explicit board-approved policies that define income and family, require assessment of all patients based only on income and family size, and describe the structure of the sliding fee discount schedule. It also states there are no nominal charges for patients ≤100% FPG. The policies apply uniformly to all patients, meeting all required elements.

compliantItems.element: Element c - Sliding Fee for Column I Services
compliantItems.requirement: For services provided directly by the health center (Form 5A: Services Provided, Column I), the health center's SFDS(s) is structured consistent with its policy and provides discounts as follows:
compliantItems.evidence: "The sliding fee discount schedule in Attachment 10 demonstrates that VHHP provides discounts and adjusts charges for patients in four pay classes: 0-100% FPL, 101-138% FPL, 139-150% FPL, and 151-200% FPL... VHHP does not have nominal fees for patients with incomes at or below 100% of the Federal Poverty Guidelines." (Attachment 13, p. 2) and Attachment 10: Sliding Fee Discount Schedule shows $0 for ≤100% FPL, $5 for 101-138%, $10 for 139-150%, $15 for 151-200%, and no discount above 200%.
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance; Attachment 10: Sliding Fee Discount Schedule
compliantItems.reasoning: The SFDS provides a full discount for ≤100% FPG (no nominal charge), partial discounts for 101-200% FPG with at least three pay classes, and no discount above 200% FPG. This structure is consistent with policy and Form 5A shows all required services are provided directly, confirming compliance.

compliantItems.element: Element e - Incorporation of Current Federal Poverty Guidelines (FPG)
compliantItems.requirement: The health center's SFDS(s) has incorporated the most recent FPG.
compliantItems.evidence: "The sliding fee discount program is based on Federal Poverty Guidelines published each year by the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, and is updated accordingly on an annual basis." (Attachment 13, p. 1) and Attachment 10: Sliding Fee Discount Schedule is effective as of April 1, 2025.
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 10: Sliding Fee Discount Schedule; Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance
compliantItems.reasoning: The SFDS explicitly references and uses the most recent FPG, as shown by the effective date and annual update policy. This meets the requirement for current FPG incorporation.

compliantItems.element: Element b - Documentation of Key Management Staff Positions
compliantItems.requirement: The health center has documented the training and experience qualifications, as well as the duties or functions, for each key management staff position (for example, in the position description).
compliantItems.evidence: "Santa Clara Valley Healthcare CEO/VHHP Project Director... Responsibilities: ... Qualifications: Sufficient education, training, and experience..." (Attachment 4, pp. 2-8) and "Biographical Sketches" (Attachment 5, pp. 1-5) provide detailed duties, qualifications, and experience for each key management staff.
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 4: Position Descriptions for Key Management Staff; Attachment 5: Biographical Sketches for Key Management Staff
compliantItems.reasoning: Position descriptions and biographical sketches explicitly document the training, experience, and duties/functions for each key management staff position, as required.

compliantItems.requirement: The health center's Project Director/CEO is directly employed by the health center, reports to the health center's governing board and is responsible for overseeing other key management staff in carrying out the day-to-day activities necessary to fulfill the HRSA-approved scope of project.
compliantItems.evidence: "The VHHP Project Director is the SCVH Chief Executive Officer. This position provides strategic oversight for all VHHP program activities... Reports directly to the Board of Supervisors (VHHP Governing Board). Oversees VHHP Healthcare Service Line Director in carrying out the day-to-day activities necessary for the Health Center Program." (Attachment 4, p. 2) and Form 2: Staffing Profile shows CEO as direct hire.
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 4: Position Descriptions for Key Management Staff; Form 2: Staffing Profile
compliantItems.reasoning: The CEO/Project Director is directly employed, reports to the governing board, and oversees key management staff in daily operations, as required by the element.

compliantItems.requirement: The health center's contracts that support the HRSA-approved scope of project include provisions that address the following:
compliantItems.evidence: "The following are current agreements supporting VHHP's service delivery, consistent with Form 5A: Services Provided, Columns II and III... Each agreement contains requirements for the contractor/referral partner to support applicable Federal financial and programmatic guidelines, including provisions on record retention and access, audit, and property management... Each contractor/referral partner works closely with VHHP to coordinate patient care, and routinely provides data to support the monitoring of program outcomes. VHHP regularly evaluates contractor performance." (Attachment 7, pp. 1-2)
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 7: Summary of Contracts and Agreements
compliantItems.reasoning: Attachment 7 details contracts with required provisions: activities/services, monitoring, data/reporting, record retention, audit, and follow-up. Contractors/referral partners and services, tracking, and timeframes are described, meeting all required elements.

notApplicableItems.element: Element e - HRSA Approval for Contracting Substantive Programmatic Work
notApplicableItems.requirement: If the health center has arrangements with a contractor to perform substantive programmatic work, the health center requested and received prior approval from HRSA as documented by:
notApplicableItems.evidence: "VHHP has no subrecipient agreements and does not contract for substantive programmatic work within the proposed scope of project." (Attachment 7, p. 1) and Form 8: Health Center Agreements, Q2a: "No"
notApplicableItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 7: Summary of Contracts and Agreements; Form 8: Health Center Agreements
notApplicableItems.reasoning: Form 8 and Attachment 7 confirm there are no contracts for substantive programmatic work. Per the requirement note, this is Not Applicable.

notApplicableItems.element: Element g - HRSA Approval to Subaward (Not Applicable for Look-Alikes)
notApplicableItems.requirement: If the health center has made a subaward, the health center requested and received prior approval from HRSA as documented by:
notApplicableItems.evidence: "VHHP has no subrecipient agreements and does not contract for substantive programmatic work." (Attachment 7, p. 1) and Form 8: Health Center Agreements, Q2b: "No"
notApplicableItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 7: Summary of Contracts and Agreements; Form 8: Health Center Agreements
notApplicableItems.reasoning: Form 8 and Attachment 7 confirm there are no subawards. Per the requirement note, this is Not Applicable.

notApplicableItems.element: Element h - Subaward Agreement (Not Applicable for Look-Alikes)
notApplicableItems.requirement: The health center's subaward(s) that supports the HRSA-approved scope of project includes provisions that address the following:
notApplicableItems.evidence: "VHHP has no subawards or subrecipient agreements and does not contract for substantive programmatic work." (Attachment 13, p. 2) and Form 8: Health Center Agreements, Q2b: "No"
notApplicableItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance; Form 8: Health Center Agreements
notApplicableItems.reasoning: There are no subawards. Per the requirement note, this is Not Applicable.

compliantItems.element: Element a - Coordination and Integration of Activities
compliantItems.requirement: The health center documents its efforts to collaborate with other providers or programs in the service area, including local hospitals, specialty providers, and social service organizations (including those that serve special populations), to provide access to services not available through the health center to support:
compliantItems.evidence: "VHHP actively collaborates with community partners (including Asian Americans for Community Involvement, Gardner Family Health Network, Indian Health Center of Santa Clara Valley, and Ravenswood Family Health Network)... partnerships with other FQHCs and community organizations... focus on coordinating care, managing chronic diseases, integrating behavioral health services, and promoting health equity... VHHP works closely with SCVH's emergency department, assisting unhoused patients who utilize emergency services for non-urgent medical needs by connecting them with primary care. These efforts have demonstrated that patients connected to a VHHP clinic reduce non-urgent visits to the emergency department." (Project Narrative, pp. 47-49; Attachment 9: Collaboration Documentation)
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Project Narrative - Collaboration Section; Attachment 9: Collaboration Documentation
compliantItems.reasoning: The application documents collaboration with local hospitals, specialty providers, and social service organizations, including letters of support, and describes efforts to reduce non-urgent ED use, ensure continuity of care, and improve access to community services.

compliantItems.element: Element b - Collaboration with Other Primary Care Providers
compliantItems.requirement: The health center documents its efforts to coordinate and integrate its activities with other federally funded entities as well as State and local, health services delivery projects and programs serving similar patient populations in the service area (at a minimum, this would include establishing and maintaining relationships with other health centers in the service area).
compliantItems.evidence: "The Letters of Support included in Attachment 9 demonstrate VHHP's strong collaborative relationships with other HRSA-supported health centers and community organizations in the service area." (Project Narrative, p. 48; Attachment 9: Collaboration Documentation)
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Project Narrative - Collaboration Section; Attachment 9: Collaboration Documentation
compliantItems.reasoning: Letters of support and narrative confirm ongoing coordination and integration with other federally funded entities and health centers in the service area, as required.

compliantItems.element: Element c - Participation in Insurance Programs
compliantItems.requirement: The health center participates in Medicaid, CHIP, Medicare, and as appropriate, other public or private assistance programs or health insurance.
compliantItems.evidence: "All Medicaid and Medicare billings, including for the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), are electronic and processed by the Santa Clara Valley Healthcare Data Systems Group (DSG). Billing for contracted third party commercial insurance is also processed electronically via DSG." (Attachment 13, p. 3) and Form 3: Income Analysis shows revenue from Medicaid, Medicare, and other payors.
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance; Form 3: Income Analysis
compliantItems.reasoning: The application describes participation in Medicaid, CHIP, Medicare, and other insurance programs, and Form 3 documents revenue from these sources, meeting the requirement.

compliantItems.element: Element h - Policies or Procedures for Waiving or Reducing Fees
compliantItems.requirement: The health center has and utilizes board-approved policies, as well as operating procedures, that include the specific circumstances when the health center will waive or reduce fees or payments required by the center due to any patient's inability to pay.
compliantItems.evidence: "VHHP's policy is to ensure no patient is denied services due to non-payment or inability to pay. Statements are sent to sliding fee discount patients for unpaid charges. Patients have the option to request an extended payment agreement, waiver, or reduction of fees based on financial hardship, which is defined as when the 'payment of the nominal fee will impact the patient's or patient's household member's ability to satisfy a basic human need (e.g., food, shelter, clothing, etc.).'" (Attachment 13, p. 3)
compliantItems.reasoning: Board-approved policies and procedures for waiving or reducing fees due to inability to pay are described, including specific circumstances and processes, fulfilling the requirement.

compliantItems.element: Element a - Budgeting for Scope of Project
compliantItems.requirement: The health center develops and submits to HRSA (for new or continued funding or designation from HRSA) an annual budget, also referred to as a 'total budget,' that reflects projected costs and revenues necessary to support the health center's proposed or HRSA-approved scope of project.
compliantItems.evidence: "The budget for Santa Clara Valley Healthcare Valley Homeless Healthcare Program (VHHP) is appropriate for the proposed project and will support the health center in providing the services in the scope of project to achieve patient projections and health outcomes." (Project Narrative, p. 67) and SF-424A/Budget Narrative and Budget Tables show total budget, costs, and revenues for the HRSA-approved scope.
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Project Narrative - Support Requested; SF-424A: Budget Information; Budget Narrative
compliantItems.reasoning: The application includes a comprehensive annual budget reflecting all projected costs and revenues for the HRSA-approved scope of project, as required.

compliantItems.requirement: In addition to the Health Center Program award, the health center's annual budget includes all other projected revenue sources that will support the Health Center Program project, specifically:
compliantItems.evidence: "Program Income for service fees and reimbursements from Medi-Cal, Medicare, and other third-party payors comprise over 50% of projected Year 1 revenue... Other Federal Funding from Ryan White Part C... The remaining budgeted revenue comes from Local Funds." (Budget Narrative, p. 70) and Form 3: Income Analysis lists all revenue sources.
compliantItems.reasoning: The budget includes all required revenue sources: fees, third-party payments, state/local/federal grants, and private support, as shown in the narrative and Form 3.

compliantItems.element: Element a - Maintenance of Board Authority Over Health Center Project
compliantItems.requirement: The health center's organizational structure, articles of incorporation, bylaws, and other relevant documents ensure the health center governing board maintains the authority for oversight of the Health Center Program project, specifically:
compliantItems.evidence: "There are no individuals, entities, or committees that reserve or have approval authority or veto power over the Governing Board with regard to the required authorities and functions for VHHP, as shown in Attachment 3: Project Organizational Chart... The Governing Board's required authorities and functions are not restricted or infringed upon by any collaborations or agreements. In addition, VHHP has no subrecipient agreements and does not contract for substantive programmatic work." (Attachment 13, p. 6)
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance; Attachment 2: Bylaws; Attachment 3: Project Organizational Chart
compliantItems.reasoning: The application and bylaws confirm the board retains full authority, with no outside veto or approval power, and no agreements restrict board functions. No co-applicant is used.

compliantItems.element: Element b - Required Authorities and Responsibilities
compliantItems.requirement: The health center's articles of incorporation, bylaws, or other relevant documents outline the following required authorities and responsibilities of the governing board:
compliantItems.evidence: "Attachment 2. Governing Board Bylaws, Article V - Powers and Duties of Governing Board Members. The Governing Board shall have the following authority as required by HRSA and consistent with state and local law: A) to hold monthly meetings... E) to approve the selection of the Program Director... B) to approve the annual health center budget... F) to establish general policies... H) to provide periodic evaluation... K) to evaluate the Program's activities... M) to maintain compliance with applicable federal, state and local laws, rules and regulations." (Attachment 2, pp. 2-3; Attachment 13, pp. 4-5)
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 2: Bylaws; Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance
compliantItems.reasoning: The bylaws and compliance narrative explicitly list all required board authorities and responsibilities, including meetings, CEO selection, budget, services, evaluation, policy, and compliance.

compliantItems.requirement: In cases where a health center receives an award/designation under section 330(g), 330(h) and/or 330(i), does not receive an award/designation under section 330(e), and requests a waiver of the patient majority board composition requirements, the health center presents to HRSA for review and approval:
compliantItems.evidence: "VHHP applied for, and was granted, a Waiver of this requirement for the Governing Board... Under the Waiver, patient input will be solicited through focus groups, patient surveys, and patient stakeholder groups. The Program will coordinate with the patient groups to provide input to the Governing Board through written reports and presentations." (Attachment 13, p. 7; Form 6B: Request for Waiver of Board Member Requirements, Q3-4)
compliantItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance; Form 6B: Request for Waiver of Board Member Requirements
compliantItems.reasoning: The application provides justification for the waiver, describes recruitment attempts, and details alternative mechanisms for patient input and participation in governance, meeting all required elements for a waiver request.

nonCompliantItems.element: Element a - Board Member Selection and Removal Process
nonCompliantItems.requirement: The health center has bylaws or other relevant documents that specify the process for ongoing selection and removal of board members. This board member selection and removal process does not permit any other entity, committee or individual (other than the board) to select either the board chair or the majority of health center board members, including a majority of the non-patient board members.
nonCompliantItems.reasoning: There is no explicit evidence in the bylaws or narrative specifying the process for ongoing selection and removal of board members, nor a statement that no other entity selects the chair or majority of board members. The application only describes that the Board of Supervisors serves as the governing board.

notApplicableItems.requirement: The health center has bylaws or other relevant documents that require the board to be composed as follows:
notApplicableItems.evidence: "The County of Santa Clara's Board of Supervisors serves as the Governing Board for VHHP. The Board of Supervisors (Board) is a five-member Board... VHHP applied for, and was granted, a Waiver of this requirement for the Governing Board... HRSA granted the County a Waiver of the requirement for the Governing Board to be composed of a majority of patient members." (Attachment 13, p. 7; Form 6B: Request for Waiver of Board Member Requirements)
notApplicableItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance; Form 6B: Request for Waiver of Board Member Requirements
notApplicableItems.reasoning: A HRSA waiver of the patient majority and board composition requirements is in place and requested for continuation. Therefore, this requirement is Not Applicable.

notApplicableItems.requirement: The health center has documentation that the board is composed of:
notApplicableItems.evidence: "VHHP applied for, and was granted, a Waiver of this requirement for the Governing Board... HRSA granted the County a Waiver of the requirement for the Governing Board to be composed of a majority of patient members." (Attachment 13, p. 7; Form 6B: Request for Waiver of Board Member Requirements)
notApplicableItems.evidenceSection: Attachment 13: Health Center Program Compliance; Form 6B: Request for Waiver of Board Member Requirements
notApplicableItems.reasoning: A HRSA waiver of the patient majority and board composition requirements is in place and requested for continuation. Therefore, this requirement is Not Applicable.
